w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: The first computer to use an inter
nally stored program was known as EDSAC, built by Maurice Wilkes, what does EDSAC stand for:
Medal in 1997 for his contribution of what is known as World Wide Web.
ABC, the first general-purpose electronic digital computer to solve large equations was built by
John van Neumann and Alan Turing
He invented the first electromechanical-punch card,
he successfully used to compute the U.S. Census in 1890
The "stepped reckoner" was the first computing machine that was capable to carry out multiplication. This machine was designed by
This early programmer was responsible for developing the programs FLOWMATIC and COBOL
Which of the following are considered as formatted scanning input devices
Hardware components that let you see or hear the result of the commands you enter.
Output device that prints characters, symbols, and sometimes graphics on paper
A projector that receives computer output and displays it on a liquid crystal screen placed on top of an overhead projector.
Part of the CPU that reads, interprets and sees to the execution of software instructions
The central processing unit component responsible for the execution of fundamental arithmetic and logical operations on data
A logical grouping of related fields
A logical grouping of records that describe similar things
A network configuration in which all computers and devices are connected to a circular pathway.
A network topology in which all computers and devices are connected by direct cables to a central hub.
To specify the location of a resource in the Internet.
It is the main protocol used on the World Wide Web that enables linking to other web sites
The rules governing how files are sent across the Internet
Software where the copyright owner grants you the right to use the program without charge.
Someone who gains unauthorized access to computers usually for illegal purpose
An icon on the Windows desktop that represents a directory where deleted files are temporarily stored.
Unauthorized and unwanted instructions in a computer that disrupts its normal operation, often acquired from downloaded software.
A handheld device which when moved on the flat surface moves the cursor on a computer screen.
Unsolicited e-mail often commercially oriented and usually sent to a large number of addresses.
A program that looks for keyword in Web pages, newsgroups and then provide hyperlink to pages which appear to be relevant
It is one of the earliest machines that was invented during the early years to speed up calculations.
This is a one-way line from the processor that handles the location of data in memory addresses.
It refers to temporary storage of data and program instructions during processing. It is also known as internal storage since it stores data in the computer memory.
This is a high-speed telephone line that can supply television, video, Internet access, and video telephoning, often over standard copper wire.

It is reengineering and redistributing software by unauthorized bodies for their own gains. They usually unlock the software by providing passwords, serial numbers, or codes required for installation.
It refers to the transfer of information.
It is a set of international standards that enables different types of computers and networks on the Internet to communicate with one another.
It is a method of translating Internet addresses so that computers connected in the Internet can locate each other.

It is an extension of the intranet of a company or organization. An extranet gives authorized outsiders, for example customers, suppliers, or business partners, controlled access to parts of the intranet.
because it does not use the computer memory to store data.
It is non-volatile like pieces of paper that can be written on, erased, and used again.
It is a network of communication lines that connects the internal elements of the processor and also leads to external connectors that link the processor to the other elements of the computer system.
It is the first electronic computer to be made operational in 1946 for the US Army to perform quickly and accurately the complex calculations that gunners needed to aim their artillery weapons.
These are the equipment and devices that make up a computer system as opposed to the programs that are used on it.
This is a multitasking graphical user interface environment that runs on MS-DOS-based computers.
It incorporates comfort, efficiency, and safety in the design of the workplace.
It is the most common feature of software programs nowadays.
The most familiar communication device to enable computers to communicate to each other across telephone lines.
The detailed program and instruction that control the operations of a computer system.
A set of generalized programs that manage the computer’s resources, such as the central processor, communication links, and peripheral devices.
A type of system software that manages and controls the computer’s activities such as multiprogramming, multitasking, virtual storage, time sharing and multiprocessing.
A type of system software that are used to support, enhance, and expand existing programs in a computer system.
A type of programs that convert the programming language instructions in a computer program into machine language code.
MS Outlook, PALM OS are what type of application software?
A standard text formatting language for documents on the WWW since 1989.
The systems wherein computers and communication equipment are connected in networks for sharing voice, data, images, sound, video or even a combination of all these.
A trend in database management that a large pool of data is analyzed to find patterns and rules that can be used to guide decision making and predict future behavior.
A database with reporting and query tools, that stores current and historical data extracted from various operational systems and consolidated for management reporting analysis.
A network protocol which became a standard for interconnecting to the ARPANET
He wrote a virus that propagates itself across computer networks known as “worm”
A terminal emulation program that allows computer users to connect interactively to a server and access remote sites.
A worldwide system that uses the Internet and other networks to distribute articles of news or information.
A short-range radio standard used to connect a range of devices into a fixed network. This is very common in smart phones.
The maximum carrying capacity of a line in an electronic communication network
An all-digital network that allows a whole host of services to be carried out on the same circuits.
A communication channel that can support a wide range of frequencies or data rates.
He devised the first calculating machine.
It is called a “bit” which represents the smallest unit of data in a computer system.
It is used for short-term storage of data or program instructions. It is volatile – meaning its contents will be lost when the computer’s electric supply is turned off.
These are temporary storage areas that hold data, keep track of instructions, and hold the location and results of these operations.
It is the main circuit board containing the primary components of a computer system.
It is mutilating the encryption of restricted databanks and databases and make unauthorized use of the information/data contained in them.
This is used in the familiar television remote control and can only work over a short distance.
It is the process of transmitting a data packet from a source to the destination via a series of intermediate stations.
It translates a numerical IP address assigned to a computer (such as 123.456.78.90) into a sequence of words, and vice versa.
It is the name of the computer or Internet provider in a DNS name.
It is commonly known as the monitor, which displays characters and graphics on a television-like screen.
It enables a computer user to enter data, commands, and programs into the CPU.
It is an electronic device that has the ability to accept data, store and execute a program of instructions, perform mathematical and logical operations on data, and report the results.
This is the first business computer which was developed in 1951. It was invented to improve information processing in business organizations.
This is a highly sophisticated and powerful computer that can perform very complex operations in extreme speed.
It is a set of people, procedures, and resources that collects, transforms, and disseminates information in an organization.

It is considered as the first electronic computing machine which could only perform addition and subtraction, and never became operational because of the involvement of the inventors in US military efforts in World War II.
It helps a user navigate through a graphical computer interface. It is generally mapped so that an on-screen cursor may be controlled by moving it across a flat surface.
Databases can be classified according to the following, except one:
The following are components of Telecommunication system except one.
Telephone lines, fiber optic, cables, coaxial cables, are what component of a telecommunication system?
These are programs that are used for creating and manipulating files to store data and combining information for reports.
These programs facilitate computer to computer exchange of messages.
Programs that are used to enter, store, manipulate, and print text to produce documents.
MS Internet Explorer, Netscape Navigator, Mozilla, Firefox are what we called as
Databases may be classified according to its structure. The structure of database that is useful for depicting many-to-many relationships.
A program that “infects” computer files by inserting copies of itself in those fields.
He developed the first electronic-mail system.
A discussion group maintained on a computer network such as Internet in which people leave messages on topics of mutual interest for other participants to read
Software programs played for entertainment, challenge or educational purposes.
The measure of bandwidth for digital devices.
A technology that uses radio waves to connect notebook computers and personal digital assistance (PDA’s) to the Internet without cables.
A virus that propagates itself across computer networks, usually via e-mail.
It is the name of the computer or Internet provider in a DNS name.
A tool designed to describe what the information on a Web page actually means.
